PARIA	ALIPOUR	

[PHONE]																				

	[EMAIL]																

	linkedin.com/in/pariaalipour																														

	GitHub:	Paria06	

PROFESSIONAL	SUMMARY	

Bioinformatics	Scientist	 with	 a	 PhD	 in	 Human	 Genetics	 and	 extensive	 experience	 in	 genomic	 data	 analysis,	 high-
performance	 computing,	 and	 pipeline	 development.	 Skilled	 in	 analyzing	 single-cell	 and	 bulk	 RNA-seq,	 WGS,	 and	
proteomics	 data	 for	 translational	 and	 clinical	 research.	 Adept	 at	 interdisciplinary	 collaboration,	 scientific	
communication,	and	implementing	innovative	solutions	that	enhance	diagnostic	and	research	capacities.	

RESEARCH	EXPERIENCE	

Postdoctoral	Researcher					
[CANDIDATE_NAME]	University,	Montreal,	QC		

																																																January–	May	2025	
•  Quantified	 STMN2	 splicing	 variants	 in	 single-cell	 RNA-seq	 data	 from	 Amyotrophic	 Lateral	 Sclerosis	 (ALS)	
which	 highlighted	 significant	 TDP-43	 [CANDIDATE_NAME]	 in	 ALS	 spinal	 cord	 conducted	 in	 a	 high-performance	
computing	(HPC)	environment.	

•  Estimated	ALS	risk	in	individuals	with	autoimmune	conditions	by	computing	Polygenic	Risk	Scores	(PRS)	and	

applying	unsupervised	machine	learning	approaches.	

•  Analyzed	whole	genome	sequencing	(WGS)	data	of	Chernobyl-exposed	individuals	revealing	that	de	novo	

mutations	are	not	significantly	correlated	with	radiation	exposure.	

Bioinformatic	Scientist	Intern	
35Pharma,	Montreal,	QC				

																																					October–	December	2024		

•  Performed	comprehensive	analysis	of	RNA-seq	datasets	across	preclinical	and	clinical	phases.	
•  Expanded	 analytical	 approaches	 to	 detect	 subtle	 changes	 in	 SomaScan	 proteomics	 data	 that	 cannot	 be	

detected	using	DEG	analysis,	enhancing	the	study	of	drug	effects.	

•  Evaluated	 the	 potentials	 to	 develop	 an	 in-house	 single-cell	 RNA-seq	 approach	 to	 enhance	 research	

capabilities.	

•  Established	 a	 database	 of	 biomarkers	 for	 complex	 conditions	 including	 heart	 failure,	 obesity,	 etc.,	 in	

collaboration	with	a	team	of	translational	scientists.	

[CANDIDATE_NAME]	 	
[CANDIDATE_NAME]	University,	Montreal,	QC									

																																			September	2018	–	November	2024	
•  Conducted	analysis	of	large-scale	single-cell	RNA-seq	across	brain	and	spinal	cord	of	human	post-mortem	

tissues.	

•  Advanced	understanding	of	differential	vulnerability	in	ALS	by	Identifying	pathways	that	enhance	immune	

cell	survival	in	the	oculomotor	nucleus	compared	to	the	spinal	cord.	

•  Applied	mendelian	randomization	(MR)	to	refine	the	long-standing	hypothesis	of	a	relationship	between	ALS	

and	autoimmune	disorders.	

•  Mentored	two	graduate	students,	guiding	them	through	research	design,	data	analysis,	and	interpretation,	

fostering	their	academic	development.	

•  Collaborated	on	four	interdisciplinary	research	projects,	resulting	in	three	peer-reviewed	publications,	with	

one	manuscript	currently	in	preparation.	

•  	

Master	Level	Researcher		
Shahrekord	University,	Shahrekord,	Iran																	

		September	2012	–	August	2015																			

•  Conducted	annotation	of	Next-Generation	Sequencing	(NGS)	panel	data	for	Hearing	Loss	(HL)	samples.	
•  Identified	and	reported	two	novel	pathogenic	variants	in	HL	and	Ataxia–telangiectasia	disorders.

preferences.salary_expectation: Depending on the responsibilities and position I expect between 70k (for postdoc) and 120k (for others).

pitch_text:
[EXCEL SOURCE]: 1. Bioinformatics Scientist
2. Computational Biologist
3. Data Analytics
4. Postdoctoral researcher (industry position)
5. Research assistant 


[FILE: clarify_Paria Alipour.pdf]
Document 1: Assignment 1 (Clarify) 

Question #1: My Ikigai 

My Ikigai is decoding the complexity of human disease to drive precision medicine. 

The central theme of my career is "Resolution." My passion lies in zooming in from the whole 
genome down to the single cell—using scRNA-seq and HPC pipelines to see what others miss 
(like the STMN2 splicing variants in ALS). My talent is the ability to handle massive, messy 
biological datasets and structure them into actionable insights. 

The world needs reliable biomarkers and targeted therapies for complex conditions like ALS and 
autoimmune disorders. By intersecting my deep background in Human Genetics with my 
evolving skills in Data Science, I create a lasting impact: bridging the gap between raw 
sequencing data and a patient’s diagnosis. 

To align my career with this, I am actively pivoting from the "publication-focused" mindset of 
academia to the "product-focused" mindset of industry. I am currently upskilling in Machine 
Learning to ensure I can not only analyze past data but predict future biological responses, 
directly addressing the feedback I’ve received in recent interviews. 

Question #2: My Ideal Day 

My ideal day is a loop of computational rigor and biological discovery. 

It starts with a "stand-up" meeting with an interdisciplinary team—biologists, clinicians, and data 
scientists. We discuss the results of an overnight pipeline run on the cluster. I thrive when I am 
the bridge, explaining why a specific [CANDIDATE_NAME] clustering parameter matters to the wet-lab 
scientists. 

The core of the day is deep work: writing Python scripts or R code to integrate multi-omics data 
(proteomics + transcriptomics), similar to the work I did at 35Pharma. I enjoy the puzzle of 
optimizing a pipeline to run faster or cleaner. 

In the late afternoon, I dedicate an hour to upskilling—perhaps applying a new Random Forest 
model to a dataset to refine my ML capabilities. The day ends with a sense of clarity, having 
turned a terabyte of raw reads into a clear biological hypothesis. 

Question #3: Job Titles 

1.  Bioinformatics Scientist: This is my primary target. It leverages my PhD expertise in 

NGS, WGS, and RNA-seq while offering the salary potential ($120k) and impact I seek 
in biopharma. 

2.  Computational Biologist: This appeals to my interest in the biological interpretation of 

data. I want to answer "why" the disease happens, not just process the data. 

 3.  Translational Scientist (Data Focus): My internship at 35Pharma showed me I love the 

space between the lab bench and the clinical trial. I enjoy working with biomarkers and 
proteomics to support drug development. 

4.  Genomic Data Scientist: As I improve my ML skills, this role fits my desire to apply 
statistical modeling and predictive algorithms to large population datasets (like the 
Chernobyl cohort I analyzed). 

Question #4: S.M.A.R.T Goals 

●  Goal 1 (Technical Gap Closure): Complete the "Advanced Machine Learning" course 
at NexaLand and build one portfolio project using a Deep Learning model on genomic 
data by April 2026. 

○  Measurable: GitHub repository created with code and documentation. 
○  Specific: Addresses the direct feedback from the Veracyte and Copoly interviews. 

●  Goal 2 (Career Placement): Secure a Bioinformatics Scientist role in a Biopharma 
company (Targeting companies like Roche, AbCellera, or 35Pharma) by June 2026. 
○  Measurable: Apply to 5 relevant positions per week; secure 2 second-round 

interviews per month. 

○  Attainable: Leveraging my recent internship experience to overcome the 

"entry-level" objection received at MUHC. 

●  Goal 3 (Publication/Productivity): Submit the manuscript currently in preparation 

regarding "Interdisciplinary research projects" to a peer-reviewed journal by May 2026. 

○  Measurable: Submission confirmation. 
○  Realistic: Addressing the "few first-author publications" feedback from [CANDIDATE_NAME] by 

finalizing pending work. 

○ 

 
 
 

[FILE: pitch_Paria Alipour.pdf]
Document 2: Assignment 4 (Pitch) 

Selected Job Offer Position: Bioinformatics Scientist, Oncology/Neuroscience 
(Hypothetical Target: Roche or similar Biopharma) Key Requirements: 

●  PhD in Genetics or Bioinformatics. 
●  Experience with NGS, specifically Single-Cell RNA-seq (scRNA-seq). 
●  Proficiency in R ([CANDIDATE_NAME]) and Python. 
●  Experience with biomarker discovery and multi-omics integration. 

The Elevator Pitch 

"Hi, I’m Paria. I am a Bioinformatics Scientist who specializes in high-resolution genomic 
analysis for complex diseases. 

I hold a PhD in Human Genetics from [CANDIDATE_NAME], where I didn't just run standard pipelines—I built 
custom analytical approaches to detect subtle splicing variants in ALS single-cell data, 
handling the heavy computational lifting in an HPC environment. 

I know that transiting from academia to industry requires more than just coding skills; it requires 
an understanding of drug development. That’s why I recently completed an internship at 
35Pharma, where I integrated RNA-seq with proteomics (SomaScan) to discover biomarkers 
for heart failure and obesity. I learned how to move fast, collaborate with translational scientists, 
and deliver data that drives decisions. 

I’ve also taken recent interview feedback to heart and am currently advancing my skills in 
Machine Learning to better support predictive modeling teams. I’m looking for a role where I 
can combine my deep expertise in transcriptomics with my drive to solve clinical problems, 
and I think my background in both neurodegenerative and autoimmune disorders would be a 
great asset to your R&D team." 

○

hard_skills_analysis.skill_name: Diplôme universitaire en chimie, biochimie ou immunologie
Expérience dans les domaines médical et/ou pharmaceutique et/ou de la cytométrie en flux
Connaissance des procédures opérationnelles standard (SOP) générales

culture_analysis.dissonance_analysis: Les valeurs de l'entreprise telles que l'innovation, l'excellence opérationnelle et le développement du personnel sont alignées avec les motivations et les compétences du candidat. Cependant, il existe un risque de friction entre la nécessité de l'entreprise de respecter les délais et les budgets, et la volonté du candidat de prendre le temps pour effectuer des recherches approfondies et explorer de nouvelles idées.

flags: ℹ️ [INFO] LOCALISATION : Information non fournie par le candidat.

rhetoric_analysis.analysis_text: La structure du pitch est claire et bien organisée, avec une introduction qui présente le candidat et ses objectifs. Les réponses aux questions sont détaillées et montrent une bonne compréhension des compétences et des expériences requises pour les postes ciblés. Cependant, certaines phrases sont un peu longues et pourraient être simplifiées pour améliorer la clarté. Le candidat utilise efficacement des exemples concrets pour illustrer ses compétences et ses réalisations, ce qui renforce la persuasion. La seule critique est que le pitch pourrait bénéficier d'un peu plus de variété dans le vocabulaire et les structures de phrases pour rendre le discours plus dynamique.

readiness_diagnostic.cot_reasoning: Le candidat présente des forces en termes de communication et d'alignement avec les valeurs de l'entreprise. Cependant, il existe des faiblesses importantes en termes de compétences techniques, avec un manque de 6 compétences clés. Les risques de rétention et de logistique sont également présents, mais peuvent être gérés avec une approche proactive.
hard_skills_gap: Diplôme universitaire en chimie, biochimie ou immunologie
Expérience dans les domaines médical et/ou pharmaceutique et/ou de la cytométrie en flux
Connaissance des procédures opérationnelles standard (SOP) générales
readiness_diagnostic.risk_assessment: Les risques de rétention et de logistique sont présents, mais peuvent être gérés. Le risque de rétention est moyen, car le candidat est motivé par l'innovation et l'excellence opérationnelle, mais peut avoir des difficultés à respecter les délais et les budgets. Les risques logistiques sont également présents, notamment en ce qui concerne le visa et la localisation.

interview_guide.question: Nous avons noté des contraintes (Visa/Lieu). Quelle est votre flexibilité réelle à ce sujet ?
Il nous manque des preuves sur : Diplôme universitaire en chimie, biochimie ou immunologie. Pouvez-vous nous donner un exemple concret ?
interview_guide.rationale: Vérifier si c'est un oubli du CV.
